verses.kjv: And Jacob went on his way, and the angels of God met him.
verses.ct: Jacob continued on his journey, and angels of God encountered him.
verses.web: Jacob went on his way, and the angels of God met him.

verses.kjv: And when Jacob saw them, he said, This is God’s host: and he called the name of that place Mahanaim.
verses.ct: When Jacob saw them, he said, "This is God's camp!" So he named that place Mahanaim.
verses.web: When he saw them, Jacob said, “This is God’s army.” He called the name of that place Mahanaim.

verses.kjv: And Jacob sent messengers before him to Esau his brother unto the land of Seir, the country of Edom.
verses.ct: Jacob sent messengers ahead of him to his brother Esau in the land of Seir, the region of Edom.
verses.web: Jacob sent messengers in front of him to Esau, his brother, to the land of Seir, the field of Edom.

verses.kjv: And he commanded them, saying, Thus shall ye speak unto my lord Esau; Thy servant Jacob saith thus, I have sojourned with Laban, and stayed there until now:
verses.ct: He instructed them, "Say this to my lord Esau: Your servant Jacob says, 'I have been living with Laban and have remained there until now.
verses.web: He commanded them, saying, “This is what you shall tell my lord, Esau: ‘This is what your servant, Jacob, says. I have lived as a foreigner with Laban, and stayed until now.

verses.kjv: And I have oxen, and asses, flocks, and menservants, and womenservants: and I have sent to tell my lord, that I may find grace in thy sight.
verses.ct: I own oxen, donkeys, flocks, and male and female servants. I am sending word to my lord, hoping to find grace in your eyes.'"
verses.web: I have cattle, donkeys, flocks, male servants, and female servants. I have sent to tell my lord, that I may find favor in your sight.’ ”

verses.kjv: And the messengers returned to Jacob, saying, We came to thy brother Esau, and also he cometh to meet thee, and four hundred men with him.
verses.ct: The messengers came back to Jacob and reported, "We went to your brother Esau, and he is already on his way to meet you with four hundred men."
verses.web: The messengers returned to Jacob, saying, “We came to your brother Esau. He is coming to meet you, and four hundred men are with him.”

verses.kjv: Then Jacob was greatly afraid and distressed: and he divided the people that was with him, and the flocks, and herds, and the camels, into two bands;
verses.ct: Jacob was overcome with fear and anxiety. He split the people who were with him, along with the flocks, herds, and camels, into two groups.
verses.web: Then Jacob was greatly afraid and was distressed. He divided the people who were with him, and the flocks, and the herds, and the camels, into two companies;

verses.kjv: And said, If Esau come to the one company, and smite it, then the other company which is left shall escape.
verses.ct: He thought, "If Esau attacks one group, the remaining group can get away."
verses.web: and he said, “If Esau comes to the one company, and strikes it, then the company which is left will escape.”

verses.kjv: And Jacob said, O God of my father Abraham, and God of my father Isaac, the LORD which saidst unto me, Return unto thy country, and to thy kindred, and I will deal well with thee:
verses.ct: Then Jacob prayed, "O God of my father Abraham, God of my father Isaac — LORD, you told me, 'Go back to your homeland and your relatives, and I will treat you well.'
verses.web: Jacob said, “God of my father Abraham, and God of my father Isaac, LORD, who said to me, ‘Return to your country, and to your relatives, and I will do you good,’

verses.kjv: I am not worthy of the least of all the mercies, and of all the truth, which thou hast shewed unto thy servant; for with my staff I passed over this Jordan; and now I am become two bands.
verses.ct: I am not worthy of the smallest portion of all the mercies and all the faithfulness you have shown your servant. When I crossed this Jordan, I had nothing but my staff, and now I have grown into two groups.
verses.web: I am not worthy of the least of all the loving kindnesses, and of all the truth, which you have shown to your servant; for with just my staff I crossed over this Jordan; and now I have become two companies.

verses.kjv: Deliver me, I pray thee, from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau: for I fear him, lest he will come and smite me, and the mother with the children.
verses.ct: Please rescue me from my brother, from Esau, because I am afraid he will come and attack me, along with the mothers and their children.
verses.web: Please deliver me from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau; for I fear him, lest he come and strike me and the mothers with the children.

verses.kjv: And thou saidst, I will surely do thee good, and make thy seed as the sand of the sea, which cannot be numbered for multitude.
verses.ct: But you promised, 'I will certainly be good to you, and I will make your descendants as numerous as the sand of the sea, too many to count.'"
verses.web: You said, ‘I will surely do you good, and make your offspring as the sand of the sea, which can’t be counted because there are so many.’ ”

verses.kjv: And he lodged there that same night; and took of that which came to his hand a present for Esau his brother;
verses.ct: He spent that night there and selected a gift for his brother Esau from what he had with him:
verses.web: He stayed there that night, and took from that which he had with him a present for Esau, his brother:

verses.kjv: Two hundred she goats, and twenty he goats, two hundred ewes, and twenty rams,
verses.ct: two hundred female goats and twenty male goats, two hundred ewes and twenty rams,
verses.web: two hundred female goats and twenty male goats, two hundred ewes and twenty rams,

verses.kjv: Thirty milch camels with their colts, forty kine, and ten bulls, twenty she asses, and ten foals.
verses.ct: thirty nursing camels with their young, forty cows and ten bulls, twenty female donkeys and ten foals.
verses.web: thirty milk camels and their colts, forty cows, ten bulls, twenty female donkeys and ten foals.

verses.kjv: And he delivered them into the hand of his servants, every drove by themselves; and said unto his servants, Pass over before me, and put a space betwixt drove and drove.
verses.ct: He placed them in the care of his servants, each herd in a separate group, and told his servants, "Go ahead of me, and keep some distance between each herd."
verses.web: He delivered them into the hands of his servants, every herd by itself, and said to his servants, “Pass over before me, and put a space between herd and herd.”

verses.kjv: And he commanded the foremost, saying, When Esau my brother meeteth thee, and asketh thee, saying, Whose art thou? and whither goest thou? and whose are these before thee?
verses.ct: He gave instructions to the one leading the first group: "When my brother Esau meets you and asks, 'Who is your master? Where are you going? And who owns these animals ahead of you?'
verses.web: He commanded the foremost, saying, “When Esau, my brother, meets you, and asks you, saying, ‘Whose are you? Where are you going? Whose are these before you?’

verses.kjv: Then thou shalt say, They be thy servant Jacob’s; it is a present sent unto my lord Esau: and, behold, also he is behind us.
verses.ct: you should answer, 'They belong to your servant Jacob. They are a gift sent to my lord Esau. And Jacob himself is right behind us.'"
verses.web: Then you shall say, ‘They are your servant, Jacob’s. It is a present sent to my lord, Esau. Behold, he also is behind us.’ ”

verses.kjv: And so commanded he the second, and the third, and all that followed the droves, saying, On this manner shall ye speak unto Esau, when ye find him.
verses.ct: He gave the same instructions to the second group, the third, and everyone following the herds, saying, "This is exactly what you should tell Esau when you come across him."
verses.web: He commanded also the second, and the third, and all that followed the herds, saying, “This is how you shall speak to Esau, when you find him.

verses.kjv: And say ye moreover, Behold, thy servant Jacob is behind us. For he said, I will appease him with the present that goeth before me, and afterward I will see his face; peradventure he will accept of me.
verses.ct: "Also be sure to say, 'Your servant Jacob is coming behind us.'" For Jacob thought, "I will calm his anger with these gifts that go before me, and when I finally meet him face to face, perhaps he will receive me favorably."
verses.web: You shall say, ‘Not only that, but behold, your servant, Jacob, is behind us.’ ” For, he said, “I will appease him with the present that goes before me, and afterward I will see his face. Perhaps he will accept me.”

verses.kjv: So went the present over before him: and himself lodged that night in the company.
verses.ct: So the gifts were sent on ahead, while Jacob himself stayed in the camp that night.
verses.web: So the present passed over before him, and he himself stayed that night in the camp.

verses.kjv: And he rose up that night, and took his two wives, and his two womenservants, and his eleven sons, and passed over the ford Jabbok.
verses.ct: During the night he got up and took his two wives, his two female servants, and his eleven sons, and crossed at the ford of Jabbok.
verses.web: He rose up that night, and took his two wives, and his two servants, and his eleven sons, and crossed over the ford of the Jabbok.

verses.kjv: And he took them, and sent them over the brook, and sent over that he had.
verses.ct: After sending them across the stream, he also sent everything else he owned across.
verses.web: He took them, and sent them over the stream, and sent over that which he had.

verses.kjv: And Jacob was left alone; and there wrestled a man with him until the breaking of the day.
verses.ct: Jacob was left by himself, and a man wrestled with him until dawn began to break.
verses.web: Jacob was left alone, and wrestled with a man there until the breaking of the day.

verses.kjv: And when he saw that he prevailed not against him, he touched the hollow of his thigh; and the hollow of Jacob’s thigh was out of joint, as he wrestled with him.
verses.ct: When the man realized he could not overpower Jacob, he struck the socket of Jacob's hip, and Jacob's hip was wrenched out of its joint as they struggled.
verses.web: When he saw that he didn’t prevail against him, the man touched the hollow of his thigh, and the hollow of Jacob’s thigh was strained as he wrestled.

verses.kjv: And he said, Let me go, for the day breaketh. And he said, I will not let thee go, except thou bless me.
verses.ct: The man said, "Release me, because daylight is coming." But Jacob replied, "I will not release you unless you bless me."
verses.web: The man said, “Let me go, for the day breaks.” Jacob said, “I won’t let you go unless you bless me.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto him, What is thy name? And he said, Jacob.
verses.ct: The man asked him, "What is your name?" He answered, "Jacob."
verses.web: He said to him, “What is your name?” He said, “Jacob”.

verses.kjv: And he said, Thy name shall be called no more Jacob, but Israel: for as a prince hast thou power with God and with men, and hast prevailed.
verses.ct: Then the man said, "Your name will no longer be Jacob but Israel, because you have contended with God and with men and have overcome."
verses.web: He said, “Your name will no longer be called Jacob, but Israel; for you have fought with God and with men, and have prevailed.”

verses.kjv: And Jacob asked him, and said, Tell me, I pray thee, thy name. And he said, Wherefore is it that thou dost ask after my name? And he blessed him there.
verses.ct: Jacob said to him, "Please tell me your name." But he replied, "Why do you ask for my name?" Then he blessed Jacob there.
verses.web: Jacob asked him, “Please tell me your name.” He said, “Why is it that you ask what my name is?” He blessed him there.

verses.kjv: And Jacob called the name of the place Peniel: for I have seen God face to face, and my life is preserved.
verses.ct: Jacob named that place Peniel, saying, "I have seen God face to face, yet my life has been spared."
verses.web: Jacob called the name of the place Peniel; for he said, “I have seen God face to face, and my life is preserved.”

verses.kjv: And as he passed over Penuel the sun rose upon him, and he halted upon his thigh.
verses.ct: The sun rose as he passed beyond Penuel, and he was limping because of his hip.
verses.web: The sun rose on him as he passed over Peniel, and he limped because of his thigh.

verses.kjv: Therefore the children of Israel eat not of the sinew which shrank, which is upon the hollow of the thigh, unto this day: because he touched the hollow of Jacob’s thigh in the sinew that shrank.
verses.ct: For this reason, to this day the children of Israel do not eat the tendon attached to the socket of the hip, because the man struck the socket of Jacob's hip at that tendon.
verses.web: Therefore the children of Israel don’t eat the sinew of the hip, which is on the hollow of the thigh, to this day, because he touched the hollow of Jacob’s thigh in the sinew of the hip.
